## [00:00 - 03:02]

Hey guys, I quickly want to show you how to essentially see where your funnel might be going wrong when you're running your lead gen campaign. So there's a bottleneck checklist for you guys to use or run through if your stats are not hitting the KPIs or the metrics that you're aiming for along your funnel. But here what you'll see is I'm inside the ad account and the first thing that I want to look at anytime that I'm looking at a campaign in the lead gen space to assess whether or not it's working is firstly looking at the amount that I've spent. So you see here I've got my rapid fire testing that was happening and then I've got my conversion objective campaign that's here. And the first thing is to go okay I haven't gotten any leads but how much have I spent? I've spent $3.67. So that's the first thing to look at before you even push the panic attack. What I would give you here as a general rule of thumb is that initially it's not uncommon for your cost per lead to come in at about three to four times the eventual cost that you'll be paying for an in an optimized campaign. So for example if I'm aiming to get leads at around 20 bucks a pot it's not unheard of that they will initially may come in at around $60. So the key here is to ensure that you're actually spending enough money to test out the ad sets to test out the ads and to test out the campaigns. So here I haven't spent much money but what I can do is I can go inside of my ad set. I've got one persona here and perhaps I have split this out between mobile and desktop so I would look at those placements as well and maybe even different audiences so it could be that even each ad set spent even less. It's either going to be that my ad loses the cent or my landing page is really not doing its job. So I've only driven five clicks here. I'm getting a very cheap cost per click and a high click-through rate. This is making me suspect that the ad is feeding out into audience network because it's extremely high click-through rate. budget. So it's actually going to mobile so that's great. That's actually absolutely amazing that it's getting a 2.8% click-through rate. It's only three clicks so far. So this is how to kind of go through your ad campaign and assess the health of your funnel. Go right down through to the ad level make sure you're spent enough money at the ad level, ad set level and at the campaign level and watch your placements and you can pretty much tell just by looking at your link clicks if there's a potential where the bottleneck might be inside of your funnel.
